expectations.text: Output classifies the task as Tier 0
expectations.evidence: Baseline does not use the Tier 0/1/2/3 framework — never mentions 'Tier'. The semantic decision (inline) is correct, but the rocky vocabulary is missing because no skill was loaded.

expectations.text: Announcement matches 'Tier 0, Opus inline. No dispatch.' or close paraphrase
expectations.evidence: Baseline output has no Phase 0.0 announcement — uses freeform text 'inline (Read + single Edit), no subagents, no review' instead.

expectations.text: Output explicitly states the transport library is not sourced
expectations.evidence: Baseline has no concept of a transport library — this is rocky-specific knowledge.

expectations.text: Output explicitly states cross-model rule does not apply (per the trivial-work exception)
expectations.evidence: Baseline does not invoke any cross-model rule — no mention. The implicit decision (no review) matches Tier 0 behavior, but the rule itself is not articulated.

expectations.text: Output classifies the task as Tier 1
expectations.evidence: Baseline does not use Tier framework. Says 'Tier-1 task' in REASONING freeform but does not use it as a routing classifier.

expectations.text: Output names plain Agent() in-process dispatch (no team_name) — preferably test-specialist or general-purpose subagent
expectations.evidence: MECHANISM: 'Inline execution by myself' — baseline keeps it inline rather than dispatching to a focused subagent. This is actually one tier LIGHTER than rocky's recommendation (Tier 0 instead of Tier 1).

expectations.text: Output explicitly states this is a one-shot dispatch / no tmux pane
expectations.evidence: Baseline says 'no parallelism' but does not articulate the dispatch shape in rocky's vocabulary.

expectations.text: Output explicitly states cross-model rule does not apply (per the mechanical-work exception)
expectations.evidence: Baseline does not invoke cross-model rule — no mention. Self-review only.

expectations.text: Output does NOT escalate to a swarm session, panes, or wave dispatch
expectations.evidence: Baseline stayed inline — definitely no escalation. Correct semantic decision.

expectations.text: Output classifies the task as Tier 2
expectations.evidence: Baseline does not use Tier framework. Picks 'Inline implementation in this same Opus session'.

expectations.text: Decision A is Claude (honors --agents claude flag from 'use claude only')
expectations.evidence: Baseline does honor 'use claude only' but does not articulate it as the Decision A Claude path — just chooses inline.

expectations.text: Decision B is in-process Skill('superpowers:subagent-driven-development') invocation
expectations.evidence: Baseline does NOT invoke superpowers:subagent-driven-development at all. Just plans inline TDD by hand. Loses the SDD discipline (implementer / spec-reviewer / quality-reviewer loop).

expectations.text: Output explicitly states NO tmux pane / NO swarm_spawn_agent / NO fresh Claude process
expectations.evidence: Implicit (it's inline) but not stated in rocky's vocabulary.

expectations.text: Output names Codex pane as the plan reviewer (Phase 2.5)
expectations.evidence: CRITICAL MISS: Baseline has NO plan review. Skips Phase 2.5 entirely. The plan reviewer pane is mandatory for substantive features per the cross-model hard rule.

expectations.text: Output names Codex (cross-model) as the code reviewer via Phase 4.3a / superpowers:requesting-code-review
expectations.evidence: CRITICAL MISS: Baseline says 'No second model invoked because the user excluded Codex' — this misreads the user. The user excluded Codex CODERS, not Codex REVIEWERS. The skill correctly understands --agents claude controls coder identity, leaving Codex available for the cross-model code review.

expectations.text: Output classifies the task as Tier 3
expectations.evidence: Baseline does not use Tier framework. Picks parallel mcp__codex__codex calls.

expectations.text: Decision A is Codex (default, no --agents flag)
expectations.evidence: Baseline does pick Codex as the coder, but via the wrong transport (MCP, not bash). The 'Decision A' framework is rocky-specific and not used.

expectations.text: Decision B is multiple Codex panes via swarm_spawn_agent / Phase 3.3 wave dispatch / bash transport
expectations.evidence: CRITICAL: Baseline picks 'mcp__codex__codex MCP tool' instead of swarm_spawn_agent. Wrong transport. Lacks the visual pane orchestration the user explicitly requested ('watch them work in tmux panes' — baseline acknowledges this is a workaround, not literal Codex panes).

expectations.text: Output explicitly forbids mcp__codex__codex per the HARD RULE (no MCP for dispatch)
expectations.evidence: DIRECT HARD RULE VIOLATION: Baseline says 'Spawn 3 Codex agents in parallel via the mcp__codex__codex MCP tool, one per mini-project'. This is exactly the protocol violation rocky's first hard rule prohibits.

expectations.text: Output names Codex pane as the plan reviewer (Phase 2.5)
expectations.evidence: CRITICAL MISS: Baseline has NO plan review at all. Skips Phase 2.5. The COORDINATION field even says 'None needed at runtime'.

expectations.text: Output names Claude Opus in-process via Skill('superpowers:requesting-code-review') as the code reviewer (Phase 4.3b — opposite-model from Codex coder)
expectations.evidence: CRITICAL MISS: Baseline does the review itself with Read + pytest + Karpathy sanity check, but does NOT invoke superpowers:requesting-code-review. The cross-model rule (Codex coder → Claude reviewer) is not articulated as a rule — it's accidentally satisfied because the orchestrator IS Claude, but no formal review skill is invoked.

expectations.text: Output does NOT use Native AgentTeam (that variant requires --agents claude)
expectations.evidence: Baseline does not use Native AgentTeam (uses MCP instead). Technically passes this assertion.
